REDWOOD GROVE POST ACUTE

CCN: 055017 · SANTA CRUZ, CA 95062 · Santa Cruz County

OverviewRatingsStaffingInspectionsDeficienciesFinancialOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
2990 SOQUEL AVENUE, SANTA CRUZ, CA 95062
Phone
8314799000
Certified beds
144
Avg daily residents
127 (88% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1970-06-01
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.13 3.38 4.72 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.76 0.59 0.86 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.74 0.75 0.85 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.64 2.04 3.01 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.50 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.08 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.84
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.63
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.39
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
57.3%
annual
RN turnover
71.4%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.20
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.87
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-06 3 1 2 16 1 16
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2022-02-23 24 14 10 306 1 306
Total weighted health score
88.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (44)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

3 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
40 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0627 Ensure the transfer/discharge meets the resident's needs/preferences and that the resident is prepared for a safe transfer/discharge. D 2025-12-01 2025-12-09
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-12 2025-04-14
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-02-06 2025-02-28
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-02-06 2025-02-28
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-11-21 2024-12-11
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2024-10-09 2024-10-11
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-09-04 2024-09-18
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-08-28 2024-09-27
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2024-06-03 2024-06-26
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-04
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-06-29 2023-08-04
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-04-05 2023-04-21
0573 Let each resident or the resident's legal representative access or purchase copies of all the resident's records. D 2023-04-04 2023-04-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-03-06 2023-03-20
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0801 Employ sufficient staff with the appropriate competencies and skills sets to carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service, including a qualified dietician. F 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0804 Ensure food and drink is palatable, attractive, and at a safe and appetizing temperature. E 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. L 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. F 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. F 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0921 Make sure that the nursing home area is safe, easy to use, clean and comfortable for residents, staff and the public. F 2022-02-23 2022-04-19
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2019-07-17 2019-08-09
